meta.title: Election Journey Guide | Conduct Secure Online Elections
meta.description: Learn how to conduct a secure online election for your club, association, or organization. Complete 7-phase guide explaining every step of the election process.
meta.keywords: online elections, digital voting platform, conduct online elections, secure voting, election management, how to run an election, club elections, organisation voting, electronic voting

og.title: Election Journey Guide | Public Digit
og.description: Secure and transparent online elections for clubs, associations, and organizations

hero.title: Election Journey
hero.subtitle: Understand the complete lifecycle of an election — from creation to published results. The essential guide for running secure online elections in your organization.

free.title: Free
free.description: Elections with up to 40 expected voters are automatically approved and proceed directly to the administration phase.

paid.title: Paid
paid.description: Elections with more than 40 expected voters require platform administrator review and approval before proceeding.

draft.name: Draft
draft.description: The election is created with a name, description, expected voter count, and phase dates. No one outside the organisation can see it yet.

pending_approval.name: Pending Approval
pending_approval.description: Large elections wait for a platform administrator to review and approve. Small elections skip this phase entirely.
pending_approval.locked_means: The election cannot be edited while waiting for approval. The platform admin must approve or reject it.

administration.name: Administration
administration.description: Posts (positions) are created, voters are imported, and the election committee is appointed. This is where the election structure is built.
administration.locked_means: "Admin Locked" — this phase is complete. Posts, voters, and committee members are fixed and cannot be changed.

nomination.name: Nomination
nomination.description: Candidates submit their applications for specific posts. The Election Chief reviews and approves or rejects each candidacy.
nomination.locked_means: "Nomination Locked" — dates are frozen and candidacy applications are closed. This does NOT mean the election is stuck — the Chief can still click "Open Voting" to advance to the next phase.

voting.name: Voting
voting.description: Voters cast their votes during the defined voting period. Voting can be extended if needed before it ends.
voting.locked_means: "Voting Closed" or "In Progress" — the voting period is active. Dates cannot be changed once voting has started.

results_pending.name: Results Pending
results_pending.description: Voting has ended. Results are being counted and verified. No new votes can be cast.
results_pending.locked_means: "Results Pending" — results are being finalized. This phase ends when the Chief publishes the results.

results.name: Results
results.description: Final results are published and visible to the public. The election is complete.
results.locked_means: "Results Published" — final and immutable. No further changes can be made.

nomination_locked.definition: This means the nomination phase dates are frozen and candidacies can no longer be submitted. It does NOT mean the election is stuck — the Chief can still click "Open Voting" to move to the next phase.

admin_locked.definition: The administration phase is complete. Posts, voters, and committee members are finalized and cannot be changed.

voting_closed.definition: The voting period has ended. No more votes can be cast. Results are being prepared.

pending_approval.definition: The election is waiting for a platform administrator to approve it. This only applies to elections with more than 40 expected voters.

screenshot_caption: The "Nomination Locked" badge indicates dates are frozen, but the "Open Voting" button is still clickable to advance to the next phase.

progress_bar.title: Election Progress Tracker
progress_bar.caption: The progress bar on the Management page shows your election's current status at a glance. In this example, the election is in the Voting phase (3 of 5 phases complete = 60% progress). The tracker helps election chiefs monitor phase completion and understand where they are in the election lifecycle.

cta.title: Ready to run an election?
cta.subtitle: Create your first election and guide your organisation through the democratic process.
